title: The Assisted Reproductive Technology (Regulation) Act, 2021
full_text: The Assisted Reproductive Technology (Regulation) Act, 2021 सहायताप्राप्त जननीय प्रौद्योगिक (विनियमन) अधिनियम, 2021 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 2021-42 Act Number: 42 Enactment Date: 2021-12-18 Act Year: 2021 Short Title: The Assisted Reproductive Technology (Regulation) Act, 2021 Hindi Title: सहायताप्राप्त जननीय प्रौद्योगिक (विनियमन) अधिनियम, 2021 Long Title: An Act for the regulation and supervision of the assisted reproductive technology clinics and the assisted reproductive technology banks, prevention of misuse,safe and ethical practice of assisted reproductive technology services for addressing the issues of reproductive health where assisted reproductive' technology is required for becoming a parent or for freezing gametes, embryos, embryonic tissues for further use due to infertility, disease or social or medical concerns and for regulation and supervision of research and development and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto . Ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare Department: Department of Health Research Enforcement Date: 2022-01-25 TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Assisted Reproductive Technology (Regulation) Act, 2021 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II AUTHORITIES TO REGULATE ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY A. THE NATIONAL ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY AND SURROGACY BOARD B. STATE ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY AND SURROGACY BOARD C. THE NATIONAL ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY AND SURROGACY REGISTRY AND THE APPROPRIATE ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY AND SURROGACY AUTHORITY CHAPTER III PROCEDURES FOR REGISTRATION CHAPTER IV DUTIES OF ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY CLINIC AND ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY BANK CHAPTER V OFFENCES AND PENALTIES CHAPTER VI MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Show Related Subordinates Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. National Assisted Reproductive Technology and Surrogacy Board. Section 4. Application of provisions of Surrogacy Act with respect to National Board. Section 5. Powers and functions of National Board. Section 6. State Assisted Reproductive Technology and Surrogacy Board. Section 7. Application of provisions of Surrogacy Act with respect to State Board. Section 8. Powers and functions of State Board. Section 9. Establishment of National Registry of clinics and banks. Section 10. Composition of National Registry. Section 11. Functions of National Registry. Section 12. Appointment of appropriate authority. Section 13. Functions of appropriate authority. Section 14. Powers of appropriate authority. Section 15. Registration of assisted reproductive technology clinic or assisted reproductive technology bank. Section 16. Grant of registration. Section 17. Renewal of registration. Section 18. Suspension or cancellation of registration. Section 19. Appeal. Section 20. Power to inspect premises, etc. Section 21. General duties of assisted reproductive technology clinics and banks. Section 22. Written informed consent. Section 23. Duties of assisted reproductive technology clinics and banks to keep accurate records. Section 24. Duties of assisted reproductive technology clinics using human gametes and embryos. Section 25. Preimplantation Genetic Diagnosis. Section 26. Sex selection. Section 27. Sourcing of gametes by assisted reproductive technology banks. Section 28. Storage and handling of human gametes and embryos. Section 29. Restriction on sale, etc., of human gametes, zygotes and embryos. Section 30. Research on human gametes and embryos. Section 31. Rights of child born through assisted reproductive technology. Section 32. Sex selective assisted reproductive technology. Section 33. Offences and penalties. Section 34. Punishment for contravention of provisions of Act or rules for which no specific punishment is provided. Section 35. Cognizance of offences. Section 36. Offences to be cognizable and bailable. Section 37. Offences by clinics or banks. Section 38. Power of Central Government to issue directions to National Board, National Registry and appropriate authority. Section 39. Power of State Government to issue directions to State Board, etc. Section 40. Power to search and seize records, etc. Section 41. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 42. Power to make rules. Section 43. Power to make regulations. Section 44. Laying of rules, regulations and notifications. Section 45. Application of other laws not barred. Section 46. Power to remove difficulties.
